summary: AI Engineering Intern and Computer Science Master's student with extensive experience in machine learning pipelines, multi-agent systems, data processing, and cloud governance.

experience.title: DAAD RISE Professional Scholar AI Engineering Intern (Pre-Development)
bullet_points: Engineered filtering and merge logic processing 1,000+ TDMS files (avg. 1.5GB each) across multi-campaign lab experiments, validating model performance to within +/-500g RMSE target.
Automated the TDMS processing pipeline in Python (Pandas, openpyxl), replacing 2 manual MATLAB workflows and cutting processing time by ~75% (2 hours to 30 minutes).
Fine-tuned a MobileNetV2 model in TensorFlow to classify 25 fabric types into 5 functional wash categories, improving validation accuracy from 66.4% to 71.5% via early-stopping optimization.
Developing regression and PCA-based feature reduction pipeline across 4+ sensor channels to support Remaining Useful Life (RUL) modeling for embedded laundry appliance diagnostics.

experience.title: Machine Learning Intern
bullet_points: Implemented ML classification models (Naive Bayes, SVM, Logistic Regression) with 71% accuracy—15% above the untrained baseline on a real-world text classification dataset.
Streamlined preprocessing pipeline, reducing sparse features by 30% to improve model training efficiency.
Created a content-based movie recommendation system processing 5,000+ titles and delivering top-5 personalized results in under 1 second per query.
Developed interactive dashboards to analyze user behavior and feature correlations, reducing reporting effort by 50%.

projects.name: Autonomous Cloud Governance
bullet_points: Designed a cloud-native multi-agent governance platform integrating 4+ autonomous AI agents for cost-aware orchestration, intelligent routing, and policy-driven decision-making across distributed workflows.
Built a fiscal governance system with circuit breakers and cognitive-tiered routing, cutting cost overrun from 412% to 0% while maintaining full task completion (80% reduction on routine workflows).
Maintained 100% enforcement accuracy across 50+ autonomous iterations with zero race conditions, at sub-millisecond governance latency (0.04ms, <0.01% overhead vs. LLM inference).
Architected a modular cloud-native framework utilizing Python, React, and Amazon Web Services to scale governance automation for over 100 concurrent agents with zero performance bottlenecks.

projects.name: RNA-Seq Biomarker Discovery
bullet_points: Developed an interpretable ML pipeline in Python (scikit-learn) for multi-class cancer subtype classification across 5 TCGA RNA-Seq datasets, applying cross-validation, regularization, and pathway enrichment analysis.
Attained 100.0% mean 5-fold cross-validated accuracy (std dev 0.0%) with regularized Logistic Regression, outperforming SVC (99.88%) and Random Forest (99.63%) baselines.
Reduced 1,000+ gene features to a 50-gene biomarker panel via coefficient-based feature selection, validated through g:Profiler pathway enrichment analysis.

projects.name: Real-Time Stock Analysis Dashboard
bullet_points: Deployed a full-stack stock analytics platform using React (Vite), FastAPI, and Firebase with real-time market data pipelines and 5+ technical indicators for multi-horizon trend analysis.
Backtested Random Forest across 5 U.S. stocks (AAPL, MSFT, TSLA, GOOGL, AMZN) using 80/20 train-test split, delivering 5.13% average MAPE across all tickers.
Evaluated 4 forecasting models (Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, Linear Regression) on live AAPL data; Linear Regression achieved lowest error (MAPE 4.24%, MAE 12.15).

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science
education.institution: Institute of Engineering and Science, IPS Academy
